story.problem: Python's argparse requires registering each argument imperatively, writing help text separately, and handling subcommands through awkward nesting. For anything beyond a trivial script, you're fighting the framework. Click's insight was that CLI structure should mirror code structure.
story.mental_model: Click uses decorators to turn ordinary Python functions into CLI commands. Add @click.command() and @click.option() to a function, and it instantly becomes a command with argument parsing, --help generation, type validation, and shell completion — all derived from the decorator chain.
story.when_to_use: Use Click for any CLI tool you're distributing or that has more than 2-3 arguments. It's the standard choice for Python CLIs, especially when you need subcommands (like git-style interfaces), prompts, progress bars, or colored output.
story.when_not_to_use: For extremely simple one-argument scripts, argparse or even sys.argv is fine. If you want type hints to drive the CLI automatically, look at Typer (which wraps Click under the hood).

alternatives.name: typer
alternatives.reason: Built on Click; derives CLI arguments from type annotations — less boilerplate if you already use type hints

alternatives.name: argparse
alternatives.reason: Standard library, no dependencies — good for simple scripts you don't want to install

alternatives.name: fire
alternatives.reason: Google's library that auto-generates CLIs from any Python object; good for quick scripting

nodes.label: click
nodes.summary: Import as `import click`. Decorator-based CLI framework.

nodes.label: @click.command()
nodes.summary: Turn a function into a CLI command. Call with `python script.py` or via entry_points.

nodes.label: @click.group()
nodes.summary: Create a command group (subcommands). `@group.command()` registers subcommands.

nodes.label: @click.option()
nodes.summary: Declare optional flags/values: --name, --count, --flag/--no-flag. Types, defaults, prompts.

nodes.label: @click.argument()
nodes.summary: Positional arguments. Simpler than options — no flag, just positional.

nodes.label: Parameter Types
nodes.summary: STRING, INT, FLOAT, BOOL, UUID, Path, File, DateTime, Choice, IntRange, FloatRange.

nodes.label: Context (ctx)
nodes.summary: Holds state per invocation. ctx.obj for shared state, ctx.invoke() to call other commands.

nodes.label: click.echo() / click.style()
nodes.summary: click.echo() for output (handles encoding). click.style() for ANSI colours.

nodes.label: click.progressbar()
nodes.summary: Simple progress bar for iterables: `with click.progressbar(items) as bar: for item in bar:`.

nodes.label: CliRunner (testing)
nodes.summary: from click.testing import CliRunner. Invoke CLI commands in tests and capture output.

steps.label: Import
Step 2
